front: What is the core concept of Abstraction in Object-Oriented Programming?
back: Abstraction is the process of hiding complex implementation details and showing only the essential features of an object to the user. It reduces complexity and allows the programmer to focus on interactions at a higher level.

front: How is abstraction primarily achieved in Java?
back: Abstraction is primarily achieved in Java through abstract classes and interfaces. These mechanisms allow you to define a blueprint (methods) without providing a full implementation, forcing subclasses to provide the specific details.

front: What is an abstract class in Java? List two key characteristics.
back: An abstract class is a class declared with the 'abstract' keyword. 1) It cannot be instantiated (you cannot create an object of it). 2) It can contain both abstract methods (without a body) and concrete methods (with a body).

front: What is an abstract method? How is it declared?
back: An abstract method is a method declared without an implementation (no body). It is declared using the 'abstract' keyword and ends with a semicolon. Example: 'public abstract void calculateArea();'

front: What is the key difference between an abstract class and an interface in Java (pre-Java 8) regarding methods?
back: Before Java 8, the key difference was that an abstract class could have both abstract and concrete methods, while an interface could only have abstract methods. (Note: Java 8+ allows interfaces to have default and static methods with implementations).
